metadata.name: zerok
metadata.description: Privacy-preserving protocol for Solana — V3 (V1 backend + V2 UX)

instructions.name: advance_active_shard
docs: Advance to next shard when current is full (wrap-around)
Called by deposit orchestrator when shard fills up (local_head >= shard_capacity).
Uses modulo arithmetic for wrap-around: (active_shard_index + 1) % num_shards

accounts.name: current_shard
docs: The current active shard (to verify it's full)

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority - anyone can call this (permissionless, validated by shard state)

instructions.name: append_vk_chunk
docs: Append chunk of VK data
@param offset: Position in the VK where this chunk should be placed
@param chunk_data: The chunk bytes to append

instructions.name: bind_vk
docs: Bind VK to state (authority only)
This explicitly sets which VK account the state should use
# First Principles
- Single source of truth for VK identity
- Authority-controlled for security
- Safe binding (empty pool or devnet)

instructions.name: close_pool
docs: Close a pool and return all rent to authority.
Requires: vault balance = 0 (all user funds withdrawn first).
Closes: state, vault, VK, ring metadata, and all shard accounts passed as remaining_accounts.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority — receives all rent

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Deposit funds into the zerok pool
@param commitment: Hash(nullifier + secret)

accounts.name: commitment_record
docs: Commitment uniqueness guard - prevents duplicate commitments
Mathematical guarantee: commitment → PDA mapping is bijective

instructions.name: deposit_v2
docs: Deposit to v2 pool with BE commitment

instructions.name: deposit_v2_clean
docs: Deposit to clean v2 pool (with optional Light Protocol integration)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State account - PDA derived with denomination

accounts.name: cooldown_config
docs: Pool cooldown configuration (optional - None/program ID for backward compatibility)

accounts.name: user_cooldown
docs: User cooldown tracking (optional - None/program ID for backward compatibility)

accounts.name: root_ring
docs: Phase R: Rolling root ring buffer (DEPRECATED - for backward compatibility only)
NOTE: This is the legacy K=128 ring. New deposits use sharded ring below.

accounts.name: root_ring_metadata
docs: Phase R-Sharded: Sharded root ring metadata (K=2560)

accounts.name: active_shard
docs: Active shard for writing (determined by metadata.active_shard_index)

instructions.name: finalize_vk
docs: Finalize VK and verify integrity
@param expected_sha256: Expected SHA256 hash for verification

instructions.name: finalize_vk_v2_clean
docs: Finalize verifying key for clean v2 (after chunks uploaded)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State account - denomination validated in handler

instructions.name: get_merkle_path
docs: Get Merkle path for a given leaf index (read-only)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State account - read-only

instructions.name: init_root_ring
docs: Initialize RootRing for rolling on-chain Merkle roots (Phase R: K=128)

instructions.name: init_root_ring_v2_sharded
docs: Initialize sharded root ring metadata (K=4,096)

instructions.name: init_shard
docs: Lazy allocate a shard PDA (K=4,096 sharded ring)

instructions.name: init_vk_account
docs: Initialize VK account for chunked upload
@param vk_sha256: SHA256 hash of the VK data (for binding verification)

instructions.name: initialize
docs: Initialize a new ZeroK pool with fixed denomination (LEGACY - TEST ONLY)
@param verifying_key: The Groth16 verifying key from trusted setup ceremony (for direct init)
For production, use: init_vk_account -> append_vk_chunk -> finalize_vk -> initialize_with_vk_ref

instructions.name: initialize_cooldown_config
docs: Initialize cooldown configuration for a pool
Authority-only setup instruction

accounts.name: cooldown_config
docs: Pool cooldown configuration account (PDA)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: Pool state account (for PDA derivation)

accounts.name: authority
docs: Pool authority

instructions.name: initialize_state_v2_clean
docs: Initialize state (Step 1 of 2 - split to reduce stack pressure)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State PDA derived with denomination - ensures pool isolation

instructions.name: initialize_v2
docs: Initialize v2 pool with BE encoding

instructions.name: initialize_vault_v2_clean
docs: Initialize vault (Step 2 of 2 - split to reduce stack pressure)

instructions.name: initialize_vk_pda_v2_clean
docs: Initialize VK PDA for clean v2 (separate from upload to avoid BPF heap limits)

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State account - denomination validated in handler

instructions.name: initialize_with_vk_ref
docs: Initialize a new ZeroK pool with VK reference (PRODUCTION METHOD)
@param denomination: Fixed deposit amount for this pool
This is the only production initializer - uses VK reference for elegant architecture

accounts.name: vk_account
docs: The finalized VK account containing the verifying key data
Validation moved to function body (AccountLoader doesn't support field access in constraints)

instructions.name: migrate_to_vault
docs: One-time migration to move existing funds from state account to vault
This should only be called once during the upgrade from old to new architecture

instructions.name: repair_state
docs: Repair corrupted state indices
Authority-only function to fix invariant violations

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that must sign to repair state

instructions.name: set_authority
docs: P3.2: Set new authority (authority only, no realloc)
Transfers control to a new authority (typically multisig)

accounts.name: authority
docs: Current authority that must sign to transfer control

instructions.name: update_cooldown_config
docs: Update cooldown configuration settings
Authority-only update instruction

accounts.name: cooldown_config
docs: Pool cooldown configuration account

accounts.name: authority
docs: Config authority

instructions.name: update_max_fee_bps
docs: Update max_fee_bps for a pool (authority only)

instructions.name: update_security_config
docs: Update security parameters (authority only)
Allows pool authority to manage security settings

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that can update security settings

instructions.name: upload_vk_chunk_v2_clean
docs: Upload VK chunk for clean v2 (chunked upload with manual zero-copy deserialization)
Parameters are manually parsed from instruction data to avoid BPF heap allocation

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State account - denomination validated in handler

instructions.name: view_state
docs: Zero-cost view instruction for system observability
Returns critical system metrics without state modifications

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Withdraw funds with a zero-knowledge proof

accounts.name: nullifier
docs: The nullifier PDA - verified at runtime in handler to avoid offset-dependent bugs

accounts.name: vk_account
docs: The VK account containing verifying key data for proof verification
WORKAROUND: Using UncheckedAccount due to AccountLoader::load() bug
Manual validation performed in handler (owner, discriminator, deserialization)

accounts.name: payer
docs: The account paying for nullifier PDA creation (relayer or recipient)

instructions.name: withdraw_light
docs: Withdraw from Light Protocol compressed account
Phase L1.3: Light-aware withdrawal with CPI integration
Consumes a compressed note via Light System Program and transfers funds to recipient.
Supports both full and partial withdrawals (with change).
See WITHDRAWAL_INSTRUCTION_DESIGN.md for complete specification.

accounts.name: fee_payer
docs: Fee payer and authority for the withdrawal

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority signing the withdrawal (can be same as fee_payer)

accounts.name: registered_program_pda
docs: Seeds: [b"registered_program", program_id]
Owner: light_registry::ID

accounts.name: account_compression_program
docs: Light Protocol account compression program

accounts.name: cpi_context_account
docs: Optional CPI context account for chaining operations
Set to None for single-operation withdrawals

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: Pool state PDA containing pool configuration (denomination, etc.)

accounts.name: recipient
docs: NOT part of Light CPI - used post-CPI for transfer
No ownership validation needed - recipient can be any account

instructions.name: withdraw_v2
docs: Withdraw from v2 pool with BE proof

instructions.name: withdraw_v2_clean
docs: Withdraw from clean v2 pool

accounts.name: pool_state
docs: State PDA - denomination validated in handler

accounts.name: nullifier_record
docs: Nullifier PDA - validated manually in handler

accounts.name: payer
docs: Payer for nullifier PDA creation

accounts.name: root_ring
docs: Phase R: Rolling root ring buffer (DEPRECATED - for backward compatibility only)
NOTE: This is the legacy K=128 ring. Use sharded ring (remaining_accounts) for production.

accounts.name: root_ring_metadata
docs: Phase R-Sharded: Sharded root ring metadata (K=2560)
Shard accounts are passed via remaining_accounts for multi-shard root search

types.name: AuthorityChanged
docs: P3.3: Event emitted when authority is changed

types.name: CommitmentRecord
docs: Commitment uniqueness guard - existence means commitment used
Mathematical guarantee: commitment → PDA mapping is bijective
Leverages Solana's account model for O(1) uniqueness enforcement

types.name: DepositProofData
docs: Event emitted on deposit containing proof data (siblings + root)
This is the canonical source of truth for withdrawal witness generation
Siblings are captured BEFORE frontier mutation, so they're correct for verification

fields.name: leaf_index
docs: Leaf index assigned to this deposit

fields.name: root_after
docs: Root after this deposit (matches proof target root)

fields.name: siblings_be
docs: Siblings array (20 levels, BigEndian format matching Poseidon syscall)

fields.name: positions
docs: Path positions (0=LEFT, 1=RIGHT) for verification

types.name: PoolCooldownConfig
docs: Pool-level cooldown configuration
Authority-controlled, tunable without redeploy

fields.name: authority
docs: Authority who can update the config

fields.name: cooldown_slots
docs: Number of slots to wait between deposits
3 slots ≈ 1.2 seconds on Solana mainnet (~400ms/slot)
Set to 0 to disable cooldown

fields.name: enabled
docs: Whether cooldown is enabled
Allows disabling without changing cooldown_slots

types.name: RootEntry
docs: Single entry in the RootRing

fields.name: root
docs: Merkle root (32 bytes)

fields.name: slot
docs: Slot when this root was inserted (for temporal tracking)

types.name: RootRing
docs: RootRing: LEGACY ring buffer of K=128 recent Merkle roots (localnet only)
IMPORTANT: This is the LEGACY_ROOT_RING, used only for localnet backward compatibility.
For devnet/mainnet, use the SHARDED_ROOT_RING (20 shards × 128 = 2560 capacity).
Purpose: Enable withdrawals against any of the last K roots, eliminating
dependency on indexer synchronization. Users can withdraw as long as their
deposit is within the K-root window (~days of validity).
Architecture: Fixed-size ring buffer (no reallocs), circular head pointer
wraps at capacity. Slots provide temporal ordering for root expiry tracking.

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version for future upgrades

fields.name: capacity
docs: Ring capacity (K = 128) - legacy ring, localnet only
Note: Devnet/mainnet use sharded ring (20 × 128 = 2560)

fields.name: head
docs: Next write index (wraps at capacity)

fields.name: _padding
docs: Alignment padding to ensure entries starts at 8-byte boundary

fields.name: entries
docs: Ring buffer of root entries (capacity = 128)

types.name: RootRingMetadata
docs: RootRingMetadata: Coordinator for sharded K=4,096 root ring
Purpose: Track 20 sharded PDAs to bypass Solana's 10KB CPI limit while
maintaining efficient withdrawal validation. Provides 12.00 bits of privacy
(4,096 anonymity set) with ~585 day validity @ 7 deposits/day.
Architecture: Sequential shard filling with lazy allocation. Deposits write
to active_shard_index, withdrawals search across all allocated shards.
Design: 20 shards × 128 entries = 2,560 total capacity
for optimal arithmetic and bytemuck compatibility. Shard size: 5,144 bytes
(huge 5,096 byte safety margin under 10,240 byte CPI limit).

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version for future upgrades

fields.name: total_capacity
docs: Total capacity across all shards (4,096)

fields.name: shard_capacity
docs: Entries per shard (128)

fields.name: num_shards
docs: Number of shards (32)

fields.name: global_head
docs: Global head pointer (0..4,095)
Points to next insertion position across entire ring

fields.name: active_shard_index
docs: Currently active shard index (0..31)
Deposits write to this shard until it fills

fields.name: _padding
docs: Padding for 8-byte alignment

fields.name: shard_pdas
docs: Array of shard PDA addresses (lazy allocated)
Pubkey::default() indicates shard not yet allocated

types.name: RootRingShard
docs: RootRingShard: Single shard holding 128 root entries
Purpose: Store a contiguous chunk of the K=4,096 root ring. Each shard
stays safely under Solana's 10,240 byte CPI limit (5,144 bytes total).
Architecture: Fixed-size array of RootEntry, sequential filling via local_head.
When full, metadata transitions to next shard.
Design: 128 entries (power-of-2 for bytemuck compatibility and optimal arithmetic).
128 × 40 bytes = 5,120 bytes (huge 5,096 byte safety margin under CPI limit).

fields.name: version
docs: Schema version for future upgrades

fields.name: shard_index
docs: Shard index (0..31)

fields.name: local_head
docs: Local head within this shard (0..127)
Points to next insertion position within shard

fields.name: entries
docs: Root entries in this shard (128 entries)

types.name: RootRingUpdate
docs: Event emitted when a root is pushed to the rolling ring buffer (Phase R)
Enables daemon fast-forward logic and operational monitoring

fields.name: root
docs: Root that was pushed

fields.name: slot
docs: Slot when root was inserted

fields.name: head
docs: Ring head position after push (index of next write)

types.name: SecurityConfigUpdated
docs: P3.3: Event emitted when security config is updated

types.name: VkBound
docs: Event emitted when VK is bound to state

types.name: WithdrawLightArgs
docs: Withdraw instruction arguments - wire format using only primitives
**Design Principle**: Wire Format Simplicity
- Uses only primitives (u8, u64, [u8; N], bool, Option<>) for clean IDL generation
- TypeScript clients can easily serialize/deserialize without Light Protocol types
- Decouples from Light Protocol's internal type changes
- Handler converts primitives to Light types at the boundary
**Pattern**: Follows Light Protocol's own pattern (see MultiInputTokenDataWithContext)
and industry best practices (Marinade, Metaplex) - wire format stays simple,
business logic uses richer domain types.

fields.name: proof_a
docs: Groth16 proof component A (32 bytes)

fields.name: proof_b
docs: Groth16 proof component B (64 bytes)

fields.name: proof_c
docs: Groth16 proof component C (32 bytes)

fields.name: compressed_account_owner
docs: Owner of the compressed account being consumed (Pubkey as bytes)

fields.name: compressed_account_lamports
docs: Balance of the compressed account (in lamports)

fields.name: compressed_account_address
docs: Address field for compressed account
⚠️ NOTE: The "new_close pattern" mentioned in historical docs is DEPRECATED (see ARCHITECTURE.md)
Current implementation: Always pass [0u8; 32] for anonymous accounts (addressless deposits)

fields.name: compressed_account_data
docs: Compressed account data (stores the 32-byte commitment)

fields.name: merkle_tree_pubkey_index
docs: Index of merkle tree in remaining_accounts array

fields.name: queue_pubkey_index
docs: Index of queue (nullifier queue) in remaining_accounts array
Note: Light Protocol calls this queue_pubkey_index

fields.name: leaf_index
docs: Leaf index in the merkle tree

fields.name: prove_by_index
docs: Whether to prove by index (vs by address)

fields.name: root_index
docs: Index of root in root history that proof validates against

fields.name: system_accounts_offset
docs: Offset where system accounts start in the full account array
Returned by PackedAccounts::to_account_metas() as the second value

fields.name: packed_accounts_offset
docs: Offset where packed accounts start in the full account array
Returned by PackedAccounts::to_account_metas() as the third value

fields.name: withdraw_amount
docs: Amount to withdraw (in lamports)

fields.name: change_amount
docs: Optional change amount (for partial withdrawals)
If None, this is a full withdrawal
If Some(amount), creates a new compressed note with this balance

fields.name: change_address
docs: Optional new address for change note (required if change_amount is Some)

types.name: ZerokState
docs: Zero-copy ZeroK pool state with fixed-size arrays
All fields are deterministic, no dynamic allocation
This ensures stable byte layout and proper serialization

types.name: ZerokStateV2
docs: v2 State with version and endianness tracking

fields.name: version
docs: Version identifier (= 2 for v2 pool)

fields.name: endianness
docs: Endianness flag (1 = BE, 0 = LE)

fields.name: authority
docs: Admin authority

fields.name: verifying_key
docs: Stored verifying key for the circuit

fields.name: vk_finalized
docs: Is VK finalized flag

fields.name: denomination
docs: Denomination in lamports

fields.name: leaves_count
docs: Number of deposits

fields.name: current_root_index
docs: Current root index

fields.name: roots
docs: Merkle tree roots history

fields.name: emergency_paused
docs: Emergency pause flag

fields.name: max_fee_bps
docs: Maximum relayer fee (basis points)

fields.name: daily_limit
docs: Daily withdrawal limit (lamports)

fields.name: daily_withdrawn
docs: Daily withdrawals tracker

fields.name: last_reset
docs: Last reset timestamp

fields.name: levels
docs: Merkle tree levels (optimization)

fields.name: reserved
docs: Reserved space for future upgrades
